_doc: Editor steering notes for the conference preview agent. Authoritative — these override prior knowledge and any conflicting context-data interpretation. Keep each note to 1–3 short sentences. Empty strings are dropped silently. Loaded by build_conference_context.py from /cfb-research/writer_notes/<season>/<conf>.json.
_usage: conference_note: 1–3 sentences with two purposes — (a) the meta-narrative for the year (e.g., 'The realignment dust has settled but the talent gap between the top three and the rest is still widening.'), and (b) calibration the agent needs to right-size confidence: what 'good' looks like in this league. SEC example: 'In modern SEC: 7-1 in conf typically reaches the title game; 9-0 has never happened. 9 overall wins is a historic season.' MAC example: 'In the MAC, 8 conference wins is championship caliber; 6-2 contends.' Combine narrative + calibration when both matter.

team_notes.<slug>: 1–3 sentences about that specific team — what the data won't tell the agent (a coaching change, a critical transfer, an injury, an off-field story, the angle the writeup should take).
Slugs match the canonical team slugs in build_team_context.py / scrape_team_context.py.
conference_note: The Big 12 is a P4 conference but trails the Big Ten and SEC in talent and funding — its CFP candidates almost always come through as the conference's auto-bid champion, not as at-large dark horses. Three storylines define the 2026 Big 12: (1) Texas Tech is the preseason favorite, but national title aspirations were tied to QB Brendan Sorsby, a portal addition from Cincinnati who's likely to miss the season via NCAA suspension due to gambling accusations. Texas Tech won the 2025 conference title and made the CFP (lost to Oregon, offense limited). (2) Oklahoma State bounce-back — new HC Eric Morris brought a critical mass of his North Texas players with him via the portal; expect a meaningfully different team than the dismal 2025 version. (3) Two of the league's top players will likely miss the season for off-field reasons — Sorsby (above) and BYU WR Parker Kingston, who is facing serious criminal charges; the agent should write the Kingston situation with formal/legal language and NOT speculate beyond what has been publicly reported. Volatility calibration: the Big 12 has the loosest hierarchy in the P4 — 16 teams playing a 9-game conference schedule means nobody plays a clean cross-section, and recent results show multiple different champions and a preseason favorite that misses the title game more often than not. When team_notes give a 3-tier range (e.g., Tier 2-4), that's not a hedge — it's the league norm. The agent should NOT over-anchor on a single 'centering' tier; instead treat the range itself as the projection. A team at Tier 2-4 means 'either it works and they're contending for the title game or it doesn't and they're fighting for a bowl' — both branches are real. Big 12 tier framework (tiers describe team caliber, NOT exact record): Tier 1 = Big 12 title-game favorite if healthy and CFP-bound (typically as the conference auto-bid, not an at-large); Tier 2 = title-game contender, dark-horse CFP at-large; Tier 3 = middle tier and title-game outsider, in the contention mix but outside CFP; Tier 4 = rebuilding year, fighting to get to a bowl game; Tier 5 = bottom of the conference, where reaching bowl eligibility would itself be the ceiling. When team_notes give a range, the agent should treat the wider boundary as the realistic downside — but per the volatility note above, 3-tier ranges are common here and the range itself should be framed as the projection rather than collapsing to a single tier. Big 12 Championship game is played in Arlington, Texas.

team_notes.arizona: Tier 2-4 Big 12 team. HC Brent Brennan went 4-8 in 2024 then 9-4 in 2025; staff is solid with OC Seth Doege and DC Danny Gonzales. QB Noah Fifita is one of the league's better starters but WR is rebuilding, and the RB/WR rooms have experienced bodies without a proven lead. OL projects as decent — two returning starters plus two portal adds. The defense is the bigger concern: four best players from 2025 are gone, DL and secondary both need rebuilds, and the secondary is being patched almost entirely with G6/FCS portal additions. LBs are the lone defensive strength — Taye Brown returns, Everett Roussaw Jr. arrives from Memphis. On paper this is a mid-level Big 12 team; last year's 9-win finish is hard to repeat with this defensive turnover.
team_notes.arizona-state: Tier 2-4 Big 12 team. HC Kenny Dillingham led ASU to the CFP in 2024 and remains a quality young coach; OC Marcus Arroyo and DC Brian Ward are both back for their third seasons. Lost QB Sam Leavitt to LSU and replaced him with Cutter Boley from Kentucky, who was up-and-down as a freshman in 2025. Lost the top RB to Texas and patched the room with two FCS portal adds. WR was the offseason's biggest investment — Reed Harris (Boston College) and Omarion Miller (Colorado) are legit portal additions. OL is a real question with only two players having full-time starting experience (one a portal add). Defense is replacing significant pieces at all three levels — DL and CB are the worry spots; the safety trio is the lone unit you'd call good. Too many holes on defense to project ASU as a meaningful contender in 2026.
team_notes.baylor: Tier 4-5 Big 12 team. HC Dave Aranda enters year seven coming off a combined 16-21 over the past three seasons — the 'what are we doing' phase. QB DJ Lagway arrives from Florida, a former phenom with real turnover and consistency issues. Skill positions don't have a clear special player; WR Gavin Freeman (Oklahoma State portal) is the most interesting addition. OL has five players with starting experience, four of them G6 portal adds. New DC Joe Klanderman was hired from TCU to fix a defense that produced the worst sack rate in the Big 12 over the past two seasons; eight DL came in via the portal, headlined by DT Hosea Wheeler (Indiana) and Garrick Ponder (Southern Miss). On paper, Baylor looks like a leaking ship trying to patch holes faster than it sinks.
team_notes.byu: Tier 2 Big 12 team. Big 12 runner-up in 2025, losing to Texas Tech twice (regular season and conference championship) but undefeated against the rest of the league. HC Kalani Sitake is a quality coach who turned down the Penn State HC opening. OC Aaron Roderick is good; DC Kelly Poppinga is new in the role (last year's ST coordinator), replacing Jay Hill who left to be Michigan's DC. QB Bear Bachmeier played well as a freshman in 2025; RB LJ Martin is one of the league's top backs. WR is the big question after Parker Kingston was dismissed from the team — portal add Kyler Kasper (Oregon) and TE Walker Lyons need to fill the gap. Defense projects as good again — CB Evan Johnson and S Faletau Satuala are All-Big 12 candidates.
team_notes.cincinnati: Tier 4-5 Big 12 team. Lost QB Brendan Sorsby to Texas Tech in the portal. HC Scott Satterfield is replacement-level at best and needs a good 2026 to keep his fanbase from fully turning. Both coordinators are new — DC Nate Woody arrives from six commendable seasons as Army's DC. The defensive front seven is the best unit on the team, though the secondary is rebuilt with G6 portals. QB JC French IV (Georgia Southern) is average but experienced; RBs are a couple of decent portal adds and the top WR portal pieces come from the FCS. On paper Cincinnati is a bottom-tier Big 12 team with a HC on the clock.
team_notes.colorado: Tier 4-5 Big 12 team. HC Deion Sanders is dealing with real health concerns; the program has fallen from 9-4 in 2024 to 3-9 in 2025. Lost top OT Jordan Seaton (LSU) and both top WRs in the portal. QB Julian Lewis played well as a freshman in 2025 when he got his chance, but WR health has been an issue this spring. Run-game and OL play have been chronic problems through Sanders' tenure. Portal restocked the offense: WRs Danny Scudero (San Jose State) and Kam Perry (Miami Ohio), plus six OL with starting experience elsewhere. Defense is a question overall, but the secondary was a real bright spot in spring and looks like the roster's most improved unit. Lower-tier Big 12 team — making a bowl game is the realistic ceiling.
team_notes.houston: Tier 2-3 Big 12 team. HC Willie Fritz is in year three, jumping from 4-8 in 2024 to 10-3 in 2025. QB Conner Weigman returns as a second-year starter — a strong mobile threat who is now an improved passer. RB is rebuilding around Makhi Hughes (good at Tulane in 2024, couldn't get on the field at Oregon in 2025). WR Amare Thomas is one of the league's top weapons; Trent Walker (Oregon State portal) makes it possibly the Big 12's best duo. OL looks strong — two returning starters plus three good portal adds headlined by Shadre Hurst (Tulane). DC Austin Armstrong is a quality coordinator but the defense needs a rebuild, especially DL; DE/LB is the strength with Sione Fotu and Brandon Mack III. Houston is a real Big 12 contender on paper — the defensive rebuild is the swing factor.
team_notes.iowa-state: Tier 3-4 Big 12 team. Lost all-time winningest HC Matt Campbell to Penn State; hired HC Jimmy Rogers from Washington State. Lost 24 portal players to Penn State as well, along with OC Taylor Mouser. Roster is a complete rebuild — over 70 new players. QB Jaylen Raynor (Arkansas State portal) is dynamic but has turnover issues. RB and WR rooms are entirely portal-built, with WR Omari Hayes (Tulane) the headliner. Spring standout RB Jayden Jackson left the team in early May, with Rogers publicly citing attitude and off-field standards. Defense looked more settled than offense through spring, but this team could be a mess in 2026 — too much turnover to project anything else.
team_notes.kansas: Tier 3-5 Big 12 team. HC Lance Leipold has done a good job at Kansas — a historically difficult place to win — but back-to-back 5-7 finishes in 2024 and 2025 have stalled program momentum. QBs Cole Ballard and Isaiah Marshall are battling for QB1; treat as unresolved. RB room is three decent portal adds, with hopes that Dylan Edwards (Kansas State portal) finds his form. WR is average-looking; Nik McMillan (Buffalo) was added. OL is replacing three starters with real questions at OT. Secondary could be the strength of the defense, led by CBs Jalen Todd and Austin Alexander. Mid-tier at best, bottom of the conference at worst — the 2026 team trends toward the bottom of that range.
team_notes.kansas-state: Tier 2-3 Big 12 team. New HC Collin Klein is a former K-State QB who was most recently OC at Texas A&M. New OC Sean Gleeson hasn't called plays since 2022; new DC Jordan Peterson was Klein's co-DC at Texas A&M. QB Avery Johnson returns for his third year as starter — a former high-profile recruit and a good mobile threat who hasn't lived up to expectations as a passer. RB Joe Jackson returns and is a good one. WR added three portal players to pair with TE Garrett Oakley, a PFF top-10 TE. OL is a real question — one returning starter plus two portal additions. Defense is also a question, though DE Wendell Gregory (Oklahoma State portal) and LB Mekhi Mason (Louisiana Tech portal) help. New HC and unsettled OL/defense make K-State a mid-level Big 12 team on paper.
team_notes.oklahoma-state: Tier 2-4 Big 12 team. Fired all-time winningest HC Mike Gundy after a combined 4-20 across 2024 and 2025. New HC Eric Morris arrives from North Texas with a flashy offense and a critical mass of his Mean Green players — including QB Drew Mestemaker, who had a brilliant freshman season at North Texas. 59 portal additions, 20 of them from North Texas alongside the staff. OC Sean Brophy was the passing-game coordinator at UNT. RB Caleb Hawkins (also UNT portal) projects as one of the league's better backs. WR room is seven portal adds, led by Wyatt Young and Miles Coleman (both UNT). New DC Skyler Cassity (UNT) is tasked with building a defense from 25 portal additions — experienced bodies but all new to OSU. The offense should be fun and competitive; the defense will take time and have growing pains.
team_notes.tcu: Tier 2-3 Big 12 team. HC Sonny Dykes is a quality coach who took TCU to the 2022 National Championship game (lost to Georgia); back-to-back 9-4 seasons in 2024 and 2025. Long-time OC Kendall Briles left for South Carolina; new OC Gordon Sammis (UConn) brings a more run-based, power attack. Lost starting QB Josh Hoover to Indiana in the portal; signed FCS Harvard QB Jaden Craig to replace him. WR Jordan Dwyer returns but TCU still needs more weapons; same story at RB with Jeremy Payne — both decent but not special. OL looks decent — three starters back plus OG Jaheim Buchanon (Florida International portal). Defense has improved every year under DC Andy Avalos and returns six starters; secondary is the strongest unit (CBs Kalen Carroll and Vernon Glover), DL is solid, but pass rush and LB are concerns. Good team on paper, but with too many questions to enter the Big 12 contender conversation.
team_notes.texas-tech: Tier 1 Big 12 team. Won the 2025 Big 12 championship; lost to Oregon in the CFP due to a limited offense. QB1 is likely Will Hammond, still recovering from a torn ACL — questionable for the start of 2026 but expected back by October. QB portal addition Brendan Sorsby is facing NCAA discipline tied to gambling accusations and is likely to be suspended; he entered gambling rehab in early May. Despite the QB uncertainty, the roster is loaded and Texas Tech is the clear Big 12 favorite. RBs Cameron Dickey and J'Koby Williams are one of the league's top backfields; TE Terrance Carter Jr. may be the conference's premier non-WR weapon. WR is rebuilding via the portal but has added good pieces. Defense was dominant in 2025 and projects to regress only modestly — it should still be the league's best by a clear margin, led by returning DT A.J. Holmes alongside portal add Mateen Ibirogba (Wake Forest). HC Joey McGuire has assembled one of CFB's better staffs: DC Shiel Wood and OC Mack Leftwich are both quality coordinators. Big 12 favorite and the CFP favorite among Big 12 teams.
team_notes.ucf: Tier 3-5 Big 12 team. HC Scott Frost is in year two of his second UCF stint — went 5-7 in 2025; was UCF HC 2016-2017. QB Alonza Barnett III (James Madison portal) is a dynamic dual-threat and a big upgrade to the offense. RB Duke Watson (Louisville portal) was good in 2024 but injured in 2025. TE Dylan Wade returns as a quality weapon; WR has some options. OL is a real question — four portal adds need to solve it. Defense is the better unit on paper, with a returning secondary led by CB Jayden Bellamy and S Demari Henderson; portal addition DE Bruno Dall (Arkansas) helps pass-rush questions. Lower-tier Big 12 team with the parts to make a bowl.
team_notes.utah: Tier 2 Big 12 team. New HC Morgan Scalley was Utah's defensive coordinator for nine years; the program pushed out all-time winningest HC Kyle Whittingham to clear the role for him, which adds a layer of intrigue. Colton Swan steps into the DC chair but will be calling plays for the first time; new OC Kevin McGiven is a veteran most recently from Utah State. QB Devon Dampier returns off a good 2025 but remains a better threat with his legs than his arm. RB Wayshawn Parker is a quality returner; WR is being rebuilt via the portal (Braden Pegan from Utah State, etc.). OL is rebuilding around two part-time starters plus portal addition Cedric Jefferson (Middle Tennessee). Defense returns 70% with a strong-looking secondary headlined by CB Elijah Reed and S Jackson Bennee; DL is rebuilt via mostly G6 portals — a real question mark.
team_notes.west-virginia: Tier 3-4 Big 12 team. HC Rich Rodriguez is in year two of his second WVU stint (also HC 2001-2007); went 4-8 in 2025. Offense pins hopes on portal QB Michael Hawkins Jr. (Oklahoma), a dynamic runner who won QB1 in spring ball but still needs to prove he can lead a functional passing game. RB Cam Cook (Jacksonville State portal) is a huge get — 1,600 yards and 16 TDs in 2025. WR finally has options: four portal adds plus some returners. OL is the offense's best unit on paper — seven players with starting experience, five of them portal additions; if they click, the offense could be a lot of fun. Defense has been the chronic issue; second-year DC Zac Alley has to build a functional unit from 19 portal additions, including DEs Ezekiel Durham-Campbell (Coastal Carolina) and Tobi Haastrup (Oregon). If the defense lifts its ceiling meaningfully and the offense hits its upside, WVU could raise its profile in the Big 12.
